steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes Theia before researchers found physical evidence of it. The word but signals a contrast: until recently, Theia was [blank]; now researchers claim to have found actual pieces. The blank must mean something like 'existing only in theory' — not yet physically confirmed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — desultory: means lacking a plan or purpose. Unrelated to whether Theia had physical evidence. ✗
  • Choice B — notional: means existing only in concept or theory, not in physical form. Fits perfectly — Theia was a theoretical construct until pieces were found. ✓
  • Choice C — veritable: means genuine or real. This contradicts the setup: if Theia were already considered genuinely real, finding physical pieces would not be a contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — spurious: means false or fake. The passage does not say the theory was discredited — it says it lacked physical proof. ✗
That matches Choice B — notional.
